requests"},{"id":3242880567,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzMjQyODgwNTY3","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/profiler","name":"profiler","color":"B3CE41","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":16,"created_at":"2024-09-26T14:01:36Z","updated_at":"2024-10-27T04:03:34Z","closed_at":"2024-10-25T22:51:28Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55889","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55889","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55889.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55889.patch","merged_at":"2024-10-25T22:51:28Z"},"body":"One limitation of sampling CPU/thread profiles, as is currently done in Julia, is that they primarily capture samples from CPU-intensive tasks.

If many tasks are performing IO or contending for concurrency primitives like semaphores, these tasks won’t appear in the profile, as they aren't scheduled on OS threads sampled by the profiler.

A wall-time profiler, like the one implemented in this PR, samples tasks regardless of OS thread scheduling. This enables profiling of IO-heavy tasks and detecting areas of heavy contention in the system.

Co-developed with @nickrobinson251.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55889/reactions","total_count":5,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":5,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55889/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2439834480","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55889#issuecomment-2439834480","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55889","id":2439834480,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6RbOtw","user":{"login":"JZL","id":1739784,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE3Mzk3ODQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1739784?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL","html_url":"https://github.com/JZL","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-27T04:03:33Z","updated_at":"2024-10-27T04:03:33Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"This looks really interesting, I'm excited to use it!

To confirm, there's no way to use this with `SIGUSR1`/`SIGINFO` signalling, right? I've been trying to make sense of the code but am unsure -- am I correct that it's this line's [1] hardcoded `0`/`false` which forces it to always be the old version?

Are there plans to make this modifiable using e.g. env variables like for heap snapshots?

[1]
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/fcf7ec081509c62967122e0949640e63a3d07571/src/signals-unix.c#L818","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2439834480/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195","id":1526050704,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5a9a-Q","number":48195,"title":"Calling `Iterators.takewhile(predicate, itr::Stateful)` causes the first non-matching element to be dropped from the Stateful Iterator","user":{"login":"ajahraus","id":9949357,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjk5NDkzNTc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9949357?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus","html_url":"https://github.com/ajahraus","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ajahraus/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-01-09T17:53:49Z","updated_at":"2023-04-27T20:01:23Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"To me, it's clear that the intended behaviour aught to be that calling `Iterators.takewhile` on a Stateful Iterator should return all leading matching elements, and leave the Stateful Iterator with all remaining elements. 

One possible solution would be to use `peek` for `takewhile` if the iterator is Stateful, rather than `iterate` as seen here,
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/53a0a69f56ded710fae2b17a33e571aa4714a80a/base/iterators.jl#L856-L861

I'm not certain if this can be applied to the generic implementation as a zero-cost abstraction, or if it would be better handled through multiple dispatch and a more specific method for Stateful Iterators. 

I suspect that a similar issue is affecting list comprehensions and Stateful Iterators. I haven't tested that myself, but in my research regarding this issue, I saw several people bringing up a similar complaint.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1526322609","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195#issuecomment-1526322609","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48195","id":1526322609,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5a-dWx","user":{"login":"JZL","id":1739784,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE3Mzk3ODQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1739784?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL","html_url":"https://github.com/JZL","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JZL/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-04-27T20:01:22Z","updated_at":"2023-04-27T20:01:22Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I agree this is an unfortunate combination of events and just hit it too (I was reading a large `CSV.Rows` in dynamic chunks. Had to use `Stateful` so I could start where I left off, but `takewhile` loses rows).

Not the end of the world because I can iterate through, `push!`'ing into the main collector while elements pass, and on the last iteration, `push!` into a collector for the next round. But it would be cool if the docs warned of this, or there was a solution. Returning two iterators (the 'taken' elements + an iterator to the start of the rest) would be nice, or even returning the main 'taken' elements + the single next element would be nice.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1526322609/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
